scenario: Tessera Bank maintains a Camunda 8.8 DMN decision table that scores 7000 loans per week against the bank's credit policies. A developer editing the input columns needs to confirm what role the input ID plays for the table and on which XML attribute and element the ID value is set.
question: What does the docs page describe the input ID as for an input clause of a DMN decision table?

options.text: It is a required unique identifier for the input clause set as the id attribute on the input XML element
It is an optional human label for the input clause set as the label attribute on the input XML element
It is a required type reference for the input clause set as the typeRef attribute on the inputExpression element
It is an optional FEEL expression body for the input clause set inside the text element of the inputExpression

a.text: Correct. The DMN docs state the input ID is a unique identifier of the decision table input, that it is required because Camunda uses it to reference the input clause, and that it is set as the id attribute of the input XML element.

b.text: Incorrect. The label is a separate, optional attribute used to describe the input; it is set on the input element's label attribute, while the input ID is the required unique identifier set on the id attribute.

c.text: Incorrect. The typeRef attribute belongs to the inputExpression child element and declares the input's data type; the input ID is the required id attribute on the parent input element, not a type reference.

d.text: Incorrect. The FEEL expression body lives inside the text element of the inputExpression and is not the input ID; the ID is the required id attribute carried by the parent input element.

explanation: The input ID is the unique, required identifier of a DMN input clause that Camunda uses to reference the input. It appears as the id attribute on the input XML element nested inside the decisionTable element of the decision.
